articles.title: Is The AI Boom Different From The Dotcom Bubble? Valuation Dean Aswath Damodaran Explains
articles.excerpt: "The dot-com boom and bust had no huge capital expenditure in that cycle," Damodaran said. "When the bust came, shareholders lost 60%, 70%, 80% or 90% of their money. The loss was restricted to the shareholders."
articles.summary: "The dot-com boom and bust had no huge capital expenditure in that cycle," Damodaran said. "When the bust came, shareholders lost 60%, 70%, 80% or 90% of their money. The loss was restricted to the shareholders."
